answer:
**Vishnu Smriti Chapter 30 does not contain any verses on the duties of the householder (gṛhastha) toward guests (*atithi*).**[^1]

Instead, this chapter (44 verses, 781 words) prescribes rules for the proper times, places, and conditions for Vedic study by pupils, including prohibitions (e.g., no study during eclipses, rain, battles, or near Śūdras), the importance of revering the teacher as a spiritual parent (Vi_30.43–47), and rituals to conclude study sessions.[^1]

**The topic of a householder's duties toward guests is covered in Chapter 67** (34 verses, 980 words), which details daily household rituals (*bali* offerings to deities, ancestors, etc.) leading into explicit guest hospitality rules.[^2] Key verses include:

- A householder must prioritize utmost care in worshipping guests: "And he shall take the utmost care in the worship of a guest (*atithi*)." // Vi_67.28 //<sup title="Vishnu Smriti ch. 67, ¶28">[p-a3a277]</sup>
- "He shall carefully worship an *atithi* who has arrived in the evening. He shall not keep an *atithi* in his house without having fed him." // Vi_67.29–30 //<sup title="Vishnu Smriti ch. 67, ¶29–30">[p-1abb64]</sup><sup title="Vishnu Smriti ch. 67, ¶30">[p-af2d48]</sup>
- Guests rank highest: "As the Brāhmaṇa is the lord of the *varṇas*, as the husband is of women, so is the *atithi* of the householder. By his worship, one attains heaven." // Vi_67.31–32 //<sup title="Vishnu Smriti ch. 67, ¶31–32">[p-571c76]</sup><sup title="Vishnu Smriti ch. 67, ¶32">[p-d36c49]</sup>
- Consequences of neglect: "The *atithi* from whose house he turns away with his hopes broken, / From him he takes away his merit and gives him his own sin." // Vi_67.33 //<sup title="Vishnu Smriti ch. 67, ¶33">[p-653828]</sup>
- Definition: "A Brāhmaṇa who stays for one night is remembered as an *atithi*; / Because he stays for an unfixed time (*anitya*), therefore he is called *atithi*." // Vi_67.34 //<sup title="Vishnu Smriti ch. 67, ¶33–34">[p-653828]</sup>
- Who qualifies (prioritizing Brāhmaṇas; optionally lower castes; family first); feeding order (Brāhmaṇas/*atithis*, dependents, servants, then couple); superiority of guest worship over other merits: Vi_67.35–44.<sup title="Vishnu Smriti ch. 67, ¶33–46">[p-653828]</sup>
- Practical duties (twice daily): seat, water, food; gifts like lodging/bed equal cow-dāna: Vi_67.45–46.<sup title="Vishnu Smriti ch. 67, ¶45–46">[p-653828]</sup>

[^1]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, ch. 30 (full chapter). [/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/30/translation/](#p-008ba5)
[^2]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, ch. 67 (full chapter). [/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/67/translation/](#p-0f50b0)
